messages.content:
Which among the following infections is not an important cause of genitalulcerations linked to transmission of HIV
a. Haemophilus ducreyi
b. Herpes simplex virus
c. Treponema pallidum
d. Candida albicans
d. Candida albicans

This is the target blood pressure in proteinuric
CKD patients
a. 135/85 mmHg
b. 120/80 mmHg
c. 130/80 mmHg
d. 140/90 mmHg
c. 130/80 mmHg

Which of the following is the initial screening test
to evaluate a patient for possible SLE?
a. Antiphospholipid antibody test
b. Antinuclear antibody
immunofluorescence test
c. Anti –La antibody test
d. Complement levels
b. Antinuclear antibody immunofluorescence test

When taken during pregnancy, this drug can
cause aplasia cutis in the infant.
a. propylthiouracil
b. methimazole
c. propranolol
d. levothyroxine
a. propylthiouracil

A 47-year-old female patient was admitted due
to a history of 1-week fever.Patient was
diagnosed with diabetes and on hemodialysis.
Patient has an intravenous jugular (IJ) catheter
as venous access for said
hemodialysis.Patient’s blood sample was sent
for blood culture and sensitivity and
microbiological studies. On the 3rd hospital day,
result of blood culture and sensitivity was not yet
available. The medical technologist reported that
the isolated organism is catalase (+) and
produced golden B-hemolytic colonies on blood
agar. CBC showed WBC= 20,000u/L, CREA=
0.9 mg/dl . What is the most probable organism
isolated?
a. Streptococcus pneumoniae
b. Staphylococcus agalactiae
c. Staphylococcus pyogenes
d. Staphylococcus aureus
d. Staphylococcus aureus

22-year-old female presents to your clinic for
evaluation of symptoms of heat intolerance,
palpitations, diarrhea, weakness, and 10 lb.
weight loss. The Symptoms started gradually
but started to get worse over the past week.
Shehas no other medical history and is not
taking any medications. On P.E., vital signs were
as follows: 90/60 mmHg, 110 bpm, 38C. She
has fine tremors in her hands. The TSH level
is suppressed and T3 and T4 are elevated.
Which of the following is the most likely to
precipitate this condition?
a. high-dose prednisone therapy
b. propylthiouracil administration
c. pneumonia (acute infection upper
airway?)
d. beta-adrenergic blockade
c. pneumonia (acute infection upper airway?)

22-year-old female presents to your clinic for evaluation of symptoms of heat intolerance, palpitations, diarrhea, weakness, and 10 lb. weight loss. The Symptoms started gradually but started to get worse over the past week. Shehas no other medical history and is not taking any medications. On P.E., vital signs were as follows: 90/60 mmHg, 110 bpm, 38C. She has fine tremors in her hands. The TSH level
is suppressed and T3 and T4 are elevated. Which of the following is the most likely to precipitate this condition?
a. high-dose prednisone therapy
b. propylthiouracil administration
c. pneumonia
d. beta-adrenergic blockade
c. pneumonia

One of the following metabolic parameters is
found in DKA:
a. Serum bicarbonate <15meq/L
b. Normal anion gap
c. Plasma osmolality >330 mOsm/L
d. Arterial pH >7.3
a. Serum bicarbonate <15meq/L

Patients with cancer who develop back pain
should be evaluated for spinal cord compression
as quickly as possible .Those whose physical examination suggests cord compression should
receive this treatment immediately
a. prednisone
b. morphine
c. dexamethasone
d. antibiotic
c. dexamethasone

Build up of nitrogenous waste products,
manifested as an elevation of BUN is a hallmark
of AKI. At what concentration of BUN does
mental status changes and bleeding arise?
a. At any level
b. <10 mg/dl
c. >100 mg/dl
d. >25 mg/dl
c. >100 mg/dl

Which among the following agents used for DM
decreases hepatic glucose production
a. Thiazolidinediones (dec insulin
resistance, inc glucose utilization, ex.
Pioglitazone, rosiglitazone)
b. DPP IV inhibitors (Prolong endogenous
GLP-1 action; ↑ Insulin, ↓ glucagon, ex. Alogliptin,
linagliptin, saxagliptin, sitagliptin, vildagliptin)
c. Sulfonylureas (Insulin secretagogues: ↑ Insulin
secretion, ex. Glibornuride, gliclazide, glimepiride,
glipizide, gliquidone, glyburide, glyclopyramide)
d. Biguanides
d. Biguanides

Which among the following agents used for DM decreases hepatic glucose production
a. Thiazolidinediones
b. DPP IV inhibitors
c. Sulfonylureas
d. Biguanides
d. Biguanides

This factor alter thyroid function in pregnancy
a. Transient increase in hCG stimulates
the TSH- R
b. Increased thyroid hormone metabolism
by the placenta
c. Estrogen-induced rise in TBG during the
first trimester and sustained during
pregnancy
d. All of the above
d. All of the above

These oral glucose lowering agents can prolong
endogenous GLP 1 action.
a. Liraglutide
b. Metformin
c. Sitagliptin
d. Gliclazide
c. Sitagliptin

A 45-year-old woman with nausea and vomiting
presents to the hospital because of lightheadedness when standing and decreased
urine output. Shelooks unwell; the BP supine is
96/65 mmHg and 80/64 mmHg when
standing.Her abdominal, heart, and lung
examinations are normal. Which of the following
laboratory values suggests prerenal azotemia in
this patient?
a. unchanged urea, elevated creatinine
b. markedly elevated urea, unchanged
creatinine
c. urea/creatinine ratio of 10
d. urea/creatinine ratio of 25
b. markedly elevated urea, unchanged creatinine

If isolated, Staphylococcal organisms are
resistant to Penicillin and Methicillin. What Is the
best alternative agent?
a. Daptomycin
b. Clindamycin
c. Vancomycin
d. Dicloxacillin
c. Vancomycin

Which among the following does not impair
peripheral conversion of T4 to T3
a. oral contrast agents
b. amiodarone
c. eating
d. systemic illness
c. eating

Which among the following botulinum toxin
serotypes produces the most severe syndrome,
with the greatest proportion of patients requiring
mechanical ventilation?
a. Serotype F
b. Serotype E
c. Serotype A
d. Serotype B
c. Serotype A
